triage.explanation: Fast route by default; no hard factual risk trigger was found.

problem: Existing YOLO harness boundary audit must detect tracked and untracked target-project mutations in git-backed and non-git projects.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
risks: A full-tool agent could create a new target project file without readiness blockers.

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

agent_results.claim: YOLO harness boundary audit implementation covers git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and excludes only the dispatch artifact root.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot function implements dual-mode detection: git ls-files for git repos, recursive walkFiles fallback for non-git
evidence.why: This is the core boundary audit implementation that builds file digests for mutation detection.

evidence.summary: gitFiles uses 'git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z' to get tracked, cached, and other files excluding standard patterns
evidence.why: This command captures the full set of files git tracks, including staged and untracked files.

evidence.summary: walkFiles provides recursive filesystem fallback with excludedDir filter for .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache
evidence.why: This ensures non-git projects and git blindspots are covered while excluding build artifacts.

evidence.summary: Combines git and walked files with deduplication: [...new Set([...git, ...walkFiles(projectRoot)])]
evidence.why: This union ensures both git-known files and filesystem walk results are covered.

evidence.summary: Exclusion logic skips allowedRoots (artifact directories): if (allowed.some((root) => isWithin(absolute, root))) continue
evidence.why: This implements the artifact root exclusion requirement from the demand.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after digests to detect added, modified, and deleted files
evidence.why: This is the mutation detection logic that identifies boundary violations.

evidence.summary: Runtime takes boundary snapshots before and after agent execution, passes output_dir as allowedRoot, and injects blockers on any detected mutations
evidence.why: This shows the complete boundary audit workflow in action.

evidence.summary: Test validates boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass
evidence.why: This proves the boundary audit is enforced regardless of agent output success.

evidence.summary: Test validates git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations
evidence.why: This proves untracked file detection works in git repositories.

evidence.summary: Test validates git-backed boundary detects gitignored project mutations outside excluded dirs
evidence.why: This proves gitignored files are detected unless in excluded directories.

evidence.summary: Test validates non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores artifacts
evidence.why: This proves non-git fallback works and artifact exclusion is enforced.

evidence.summary: Doctrine states dispatch runs boundary audits before/after agent execution and blockers any target-project mutation while only allowing artifact root writes
evidence.why: This is the product requirement specification for boundary audit behavior.

evidence.summary: Stress test scenario 'git-untracked-boundary' validates the exact claim with objective matching the demand text
evidence.why: This shows the demand is being actively stress-tested against real Claude agents.

risks: If boundary snapshot logic has gaps, full-tool agents could mutate project files without triggering blockers
If git ls-files flags don't cover all file states, some mutations might go undetected
If excludedDirs list is incomplete, build artifacts could create false positives or performance issues

result.notes: Implementation matches acceptance criteria: boundary snapshots use git ls-files for tracked/cached/other files, walkFiles fallback for non-git, and exclude only the dispatch artifact root. Test coverage validates all mutation types across both git and non-git scenarios.

agent_results.claim: YOLO harness boundary audit implementation covers git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and excludes only the dispatch artifact root.

evidence.summary: gitFiles function uses 'git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z' covering tracked (-c), cached (-o), and other files while excluding standard patterns
evidence.why: Verifies the exact git command coverage claimed in the acceptance criteria.

evidence.summary: walkFiles fallback provides recursive filesystem coverage with excludedDir filter for build artifacts
evidence.why: Confirms fallback walk requirement exists for non-git projects and git blindspots.

evidence.summary: Union of git and walked files with Set deduplication ensures combined coverage
evidence.why: Proves both detection modes are used together as specified.

evidence.summary: allowedRoots exclusion logic skips artifact directories with isWithin check
evidence.why: Verifies dispatch artifact root exclusion implementation.

evidence.summary: Runtime workflow shows before/after snapshots and output_dir passed as allowedRoot
evidence.why: Confirms the complete boundary audit is wired into the agent dispatch.

evidence.summary: Four test cases cover: tracked file mutation, untracked file detection in git, gitignored file detection, and non-git fallback with artifact exclusion
evidence.why: Validates all acceptance criteria scenarios have test coverage.

evidence.summary: Doctrine specification matches implementation: boundary audit before/after agent execution, mutation blockers, artifact root writes allowed
evidence.why: Confirms product requirement alignment.

risks: The git ls-files command may not cover all edge cases in non-standard git repositories
WalkFiles fallback may miss files if excludedDirs list is incomplete for project-specific build directories
Race condition risk if target project is mutated by external processes during snapshot window

result.notes: Explorer evidence is sufficient and properly scoped. All project facts include repo-relative paths and line numbers. No assumptions promoted to facts. Evidence directly supports the acceptance criteria: git ls-files for tracked/cached/other files, walkFiles fallback, artifact root exclusion. Test coverage validates all mutation detection paths. Implementation aligns with doctrine specification.
